# UC-17.3.39 · FortiSASE SWG Policy Violation Trends (Fortinet)

## Description

FortiSASE cloud-delivered SWG enforces web filtering for remote and branch users through FortiGate NGFW policies in the cloud. Trending blocked categories and rules identifies policy drift and shadow IT.

## Value

FortiSASE cloud-delivered SWG enforces web filtering for remote and branch users through FortiGate NGFW policies in the cloud. Trending blocked categories and rules identifies policy drift and shadow IT.

## Implementation

Forward FortiSASE logs via syslog to Splunk HF. The `TA-fortinet_fortigate` parses FortiSASE traffic identically to on-prem FortiGate. Alert when daily blocks exceed 2× 7-day baseline. Segment by FortiSASE PoP region for regional policy analysis.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE logs use FortiGate syslog format).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=fgt_utm` (FortiSASE web filter logs via syslog).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward FortiSASE logs via syslog to Splunk HF. The `TA-fortinet_fortigate` parses FortiSASE traffic identically to on-prem FortiGate. Alert when daily blocks exceed 2× 7-day baseline. Segment by FortiSASE PoP region for regional policy analysis.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="fgt_utm" subtype="webfilter" earliest=-7d
| where action="blocked"
| stats count by catdesc, user, policyname
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**FortiSASE SWG Policy Violation Trends (Fortinet)** — FortiSASE cloud-delivered SWG enforces web filtering for remote and branch users through FortiGate NGFW policies in the cloud. Trending blocked categories and rules identifies policy drift and shadow IT.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=fgt_utm` (FortiSASE web filter logs via syslog). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA-fortinet_fortigate` (FortiSASE logs use FortiGate syslog format). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: proxy; **sourcetype**: fgt_utm. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=proxy, sourcetype="fgt_utm", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where action="blocked"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by catdesc, user, policyname** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with FortiGate/FortiSASE manager for the same policies, users, and time window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top blocked users), Line chart (daily block trend), Pie chart (category distribution).

## SPL

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="fgt_utm" subtype="webfilter" earliest=-7d
| where action="blocked"
| stats count by catdesc, user, policyname
| sort -count
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic
  by All_Traffic.user, All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (blocks by category), Table (top blocked users), Line chart (daily block trend), Pie chart (category distribution).
